Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Defendants, File Proofs Of Service Per Ccp And Failure Top Rosecute The Action
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BA MINUZZI'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO SERVE DEFENDANTS, FILE PROOFS OF SERVICE PER CCP AND FAILURE TO PROSECUTE THE ACTION

Appearances:
Lee Sacks (310.451.3113) for plaintiff and opposing party. No other appearances.

The court did not receive notice of defendant's intent to argue against the tentative ruling. Discussed and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Ba Minuzzi's motion to dismiss for failure to serve defendants and failure to prosecute the action is denied. The court lacks statutory or rule authority to dismiss a complaint due to asserted delay of less than two years. (Holden v. California Employment Stabilization Commission (1950) 101 Cal. App. 2d 427, 437-438). While the court has inherent authority to dismiss under certain circumstances, it would be an abuse of discretion to do so at this time in this case based on the record before the court. Counsel for the moving party is strongly admonished not to file papers with this or any other court with egregious miscitations of authority such as the demonstrably false quotation on line 1 of page 7 of the memorandum and not to file a declaration largely comprised of argument and inadmissible purported facts. In addition, the motion was not served and filed in compliance with CCP 1005(b) which requires that an additional ten days be added when, as here, the motion papers are served by mail and the place of mailing is outside California. The court will issue an order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
